Developer Reference

Overview

documents

databases

me

users

groups

blob-buckets

workflows

analytics

On this page

me — client.me ​

The signed-in user: read and update their profile, list the documents they own or that are shared with them, manage pending invitations, and control the profile cache. To look up other users, see users.

Divergent shape

Most of client.me matches by name across both clients, but the Swift surface is largely untyped: get/update/uploadAvatar/pendingDocumentInvitations return (and update accepts) [String: Any] where JS uses named interfaces (UserProfile, UpdateMeParams, …). ownedDocuments/sharedDocuments return the raw { items, cursor? } envelope as a dictionary, and Swift's ownedDocuments is a bare network GET that accepts only cursor/limit/tag — it drops the 7 offline-first option fields JS exposes and does no local-cache merge. Both compile; the shapes differ (#938, #954).

get(options?) ​

Read the signed-in user's profile, using the cache when available. Returns null if there is no current user.

ts
const profile = await client.me.get({
  waitForLoad: "localIfAvailableElseNetwork",
  refreshIfOlderThanMs: 60_000,
});
swift
let profile = try await client.me.get(options: FetchCachedOptions(
  waitForLoad: .localIfAvailableElseNetwork,
  refreshIfOlderThanMs: 60_000
))

update(params) ​

Update the profile's name and/or avatarUrl. Pass avatarUrl: null (JS) to clear the avatar — Swift takes an untyped [String: Any], so use NSNull() for the same effect (#954).

ts
const profile = await client.me.update({ name, avatarUrl: null });
swift
let profile = try await client.me.update(params: [
  "name": name,
  "avatarUrl": NSNull(),
])

uploadAvatar(imageData, contentType) ​

Upload an avatar image and get back the new avatar URL. In JS contentType is a typed union (image/png | image/jpeg | image/gif | image/webp); Swift takes a bare String, so an invalid MIME type fails at runtime rather than compile-time (#954).

ts
const { avatarUrl } = await client.me.uploadAvatar(image, "image/png");
swift
let result = try await client.me.uploadAvatar(imageData: image, contentType: "image/png")
let avatarUrl = result["avatarUrl"] as? String

ownedDocuments(options?) ​

List documents the current user owns (live ownership, not creator). JS returns a flat DocumentInfo[] by default (or a { items, cursor } page with returnPage: true) and accepts the full documents.list option set.

Divergent shape

Swift's ownedDocuments accepts only cursor/limit/tag and returns the raw { items, cursor? } envelope as [String: Any]. The 7 offline-first knobs (includeRoot, refreshFromServer, localOnly, serverTimeoutMs, waitForLoad, forward, returnPage) are absent, and the Swift call is a bare network GET with no local-cache merge or stale-refresh (#938, #954).

ts
const docs = await client.me.ownedDocuments({
  limit: 50,
  tag: "project",
  includeRoot: false,
  localOnly: false,
});
swift
let result = try await client.me.ownedDocuments(limit: 50, tag: "project")
let items = result["items"] as? [[String: Any]] ?? []

sharedDocuments(options?) ​

List documents shared with the current user (non-owner permissions plus pending legacy invitations). Returns the unified { items, cursor? } envelope; pass tag to filter and cursor to paginate.

Divergent shape

Swift returns the envelope as an untyped [String: Any] and, like ownedDocuments, is a bare network GET (#938, #954).

ts
const { items, cursor } = await client.me.sharedDocuments({
  limit: 50,
  tag: "shared",
});
swift
let result = try await client.me.sharedDocuments(limit: 50, tag: "shared")
let items = result["items"] as? [[String: Any]] ?? []
let cursor = result["cursor"] as? String

pendingDocumentInvitations() ​

List pending document invitations for the current user. JS returns a typed array (invitationId, documentId, permission, accepted, nested document, …); Swift returns [[String: Any]] (#954).

ts
const invitations = await client.me.pendingDocumentInvitations();
for (const invite of invitations) {
  console.log(invite.invitationId, invite.documentId, invite.permission);
}
swift
let invitations = try await client.me.pendingDocumentInvitations()
for invite in invitations {
  let id = invite["invitationId"] as? String
  let permission = invite["permission"] as? String
  print(id ?? "", permission ?? "")
}

cacheInfo() ​

Read cache metadata for the current user's profile entry. JS returns { updatedAt?, ageMs? }; Swift returns the typed tuple (updatedAt: String?, ageMs: Double?).

ts
const { updatedAt, ageMs } = await client.me.cacheInfo();
swift
let info = await client.me.cacheInfo()
let updatedAt = info.updatedAt
let ageMs = info.ageMs

clearCache() ​

Clear the cached profile so the next get() fetches fresh data from the server.

ts
await client.me.clearCache();
swift
await client.me.clearCache()
